text.title: Image Generation Rules
text.summary: Rate-limit, URL expiry, revised_prompt logging, content policy, and provider selection rules.

rule.statement: Always log revised_prompt alongside the original prompt for every DALL-E 3 generation.
rule.rationale: DALL-E 3 silently rewrites prompts — revised_prompt can diverge substantially from input. Downstream vision review agents must compare against the revised prompt, not the original.

rule.statement: Download images immediately after generation. Use content-based (SHA-256 of bytes) cache with S3 or Redis as backend — never cache provider URLs.
rule.rationale: DALL-E pre-signed URLs expire in approximately 60 minutes. File-based URL caches go stale; agents resuming after 1+ hours get 404 on all cached entries.

rule.statement: Test prompts manually on the first 3 before launching a batch of 100+ images.
rule.rationale: Content policy enforcement is not deterministic. A prompt that passes today may be rejected next week. Early validation prevents wasted batch budget.

rule.statement: Use Replicate Flux-schnell for high-volume generation where cost is the primary constraint. Use DALL-E 3 for photorealistic quality. Never use Midjourney in automated pipelines — automation violates ToS.
rule.rationale: DALL-E 3 at $0.04-$0.12/image accumulates quickly at scale. Flux-schnell at ~$0.001/image is 40-120x cheaper with acceptable quality for social content.

rule.statement: Handle content policy refusals gracefully without infinite retry. Log the prompt and error, then surface for human review.
rule.rationale: Content policy decisions are not deterministic — retrying the exact same prompt will eventually pass or fail differently, wasting API budget. Human review is the correct escalation path.

antipattern.description: Expecting DALL-E 3 to render precise text within the image.
antipattern.reason: All current models including DALL-E 3 are unreliable for rendering text strings inside images — characters are frequently garbled or misspelled.
